initial_signal: Candidate receives an official-looking IBM recruitment email followed within minutes by a Telegram message from the same recruiter requesting a ₹1500 refundable interview token before scheduling.

sender_profile.name: Priya Nair
sender_profile.notes: Email domain is legitimate IBM but Telegram handle is newly created and unverified. Email and Telegram personas show inconsistent profile details — LinkedIn shows no IBM employment history.

organization_profile.name: IBM
organization_profile.notes: IBM's official policy explicitly prohibits requesting fees or deposits at any stage of hiring. IBM does not use Telegram for official recruitment communications.

shared_channel_history: LinkedIn post from IBM's verified page confirms active lateral hiring drive in Bangalore (Apr 2026).
Three candidates in a #ibm-careers Slack community independently reported receiving identical Telegram follow-ups after IBM email outreach — all requesting refundable tokens.
IBM's verified LinkedIn account lists no recruiter named Priya Nair.
Community moderator flagged @priya_ibm_fast as suspicious 6 days ago after two members reported unsolicited payment requests.
private_conversation_history: Telegram [Day 0, 14:02]: Hi! I am Priya from IBM Talent Acquisition. Congratulations on clearing the initial screening!
Telegram [Day 0, 14:03]: To reserve your interview slot a refundable token of ₹1500 must be deposited via UPI to priya.ibm.pay@upi
Telegram [Day 0, 14:05]: This amount is fully refundable within 48 hours of your interview regardless of outcome. Slots are filling fast!
Telegram [Day 0, 14:09]: Payment link: [bit.ly/ibm-token-pay]. Please complete within 30 minutes to secure your slot.
Candidate [Day 0, 14:12]: Can you verify this through the IBM careers portal?
Telegram [Day 0, 14:13]: Our portal is under maintenance right now. This is the only way to book your slot. Please hurry or your slot will be released.

candidate_interaction_history.details: Email from priya.nair@ibm.com with IBM branding referenced candidate LinkedIn profile and congratulated on profile shortlist.
Email contained OA link hosted on careers.ibm.com — link is valid and resolves correctly.
Telegram contact arrived 4.5 hours after email asking for ₹1500 payment before any interview was scheduled.

external_market_signals: Telegram handle @priya_ibm_fast appears in 3 separate scam complaint threads on reddit.com/r/india and Glassdoor forums between March and April 2026.
UPI ID priya.ibm.pay@upi is flagged by cybercrime.gov.in as associated with an active job scam cluster targeting IT job seekers.
Shortened URL bit.ly/ibm-token-pay resolves to a third-party phishing payment page with no IBM affiliation.
No individual named Priya Nair appears in IBM's verified recruiter directory on LinkedIn or careers.ibm.com.

anomalies: Font inconsistency on page 2 mixing Times New Roman with Calibri
IBM logo is low resolution consistent with a web screenshot paste
Signatory listed as Ramesh Gupta VP Talent who does not appear in IBM leadership directory
Document metadata shows creation via LibreOffice 7.4 not IBM standard toolchain
No IBM document ID HR reference number or DocuSign envelope present

temporal_context.notes: A 4.5-hour gap between the legitimate-looking email and the Telegram payment request is consistent with coordinated cross-platform social engineering. The 30-minute payment window is a hallmark pressure tactic used in advance-fee fraud.

missing_verification_signals: No response from IBM HR when candidate emailed hr-verify@ibm.com
IBM careers portal shows no active application for this candidate

initial_signal: Candidate receives an official email from careers@adobe.com congratulating them on a shortlist and asking them to purchase a MacBook Pro from an approved vendor before their start date, with the invoice to be reimbursed on joining.

sender_profile.name: Sarah Mitchell
anomalies: WhatsApp number is an unregistered Indian mobile; Adobe's global HR uses internal VOIP or email only
Email header analysis shows originating IP is a residential ISP in Pune, not Adobe's mail infrastructure
DKIM signature passes for adobe.com domain but SPF soft-fail present — consistent with mailbox compromise rather than spoofing
sender_profile.notes: The adobe.com domain is genuine which makes this high-risk. Evidence points to a compromised internal mailbox being weaponised rather than a spoofed sender address.

organization_profile.name: Adobe
organization_profile.equipment_purchase_policy: Adobe ships equipment directly to new hires via internal IT — candidates are never asked to self-purchase hardware
organization_profile.notes: Adobe's published fraud notice explicitly states the company never asks candidates to purchase equipment. Equipment provisioning is handled by IT after offer acceptance via official portal only.

shared_channel_history: Adobe's official LinkedIn page has no open role matching the description sent to the candidate.
Two candidates in an Adobe interview prep Discord server reported receiving identical equipment purchase requests from careers@adobe.com in the past 10 days.
Adobe's verified Twitter/X account posted a fraud alert on 2026-03-28 warning of compromised recruiter mailbox activity.
A cybersecurity blog published an article on 2026-03-30 detailing a BEC campaign targeting job seekers using compromised Adobe recruiter accounts.
private_conversation_history: WhatsApp [Day 0, 11:15]: Hi! This is Sarah from Adobe HR. Congratulations — you have been selected for the Senior Designer role!

WhatsApp [Day 0, 11:17]: As part of onboarding Adobe provides a MacBook Pro. However due to logistics delays in your region you will need to purchase it from our approved vendor and Adobe will reimburse on Day 1.
WhatsApp [Day 0, 11:18]: Vendor payment link: [bit.ly/adobe-vendor-mbp]. Total: ₹1,45,000. Please complete by EOD today.
WhatsApp [Day 0, 11:20]: Once payment is done please share screenshot here and your offer letter will be released.
Candidate [Day 0, 11:35]: Can I verify this through the Adobe careers portal or Workday?
WhatsApp [Day 0, 11:36]: The Workday portal is only activated post offer. This is standard pre-boarding. Please proceed.
WhatsApp [Day 0, 11:40]: I can arrange a quick call if you need reassurance. Time is limited as we have other candidates waiting.

candidate_interaction_history.details: Candidate applied via Adobe Workday portal for Senior UX Designer — application status shows Under Review with no interview scheduled.
Email from careers@adobe.com arrived without any prior interview steps, congratulating candidate on shortlist and attaching a vendor invoice.
WhatsApp follow-up arrived 75 minutes after email pushing for same-day payment of ₹1,45,000 for hardware purchase.

external_market_signals: Adobe's fraud notice page confirms an active BEC campaign involving compromised recruiter mailboxes as of March 2026.
The vendor payment URL bit.ly/adobe-vendor-mbp redirects to a Razorpay-lookalike page with no registered business name.
cybercrime.gov.in lists the associated UPI handle as part of a BEC job-scam cluster active since February 2026.
No job reference number exists on Adobe's Workday portal matching the role described in the email.

anomalies: Vendor name is TechZone Supplies Pvt Ltd — not a registered Apple Authorised Reseller
GST number on invoice fails validation on the GST portal
Invoice template matches a freely available Canva invoice design
Adobe logo is low resolution and cropped inconsistently with authentic Adobe documents
No Adobe PO number or finance reference present
Offer is contingent on hardware purchase completion — no legitimate offer letter contains this clause
Signatory is listed as David Park VP People — LinkedIn confirms David Park left Adobe in 2024
Document has no DocuSign envelope ID or Adobe Sign tracking reference
Salary figure is 40 percent above market rate for the role — consistent with bait-and-switch fraud
Metadata shows document created in Canva not Adobe HR toolchain

temporal_context.notes: The 75-minute gap between the email and WhatsApp contact combined with a same-day payment deadline of ₹1,45,000 is consistent with a Business Email Compromise playbook. DKIM passes but SPF soft-fails — strong indicator of mailbox compromise rather than domain spoofing.

initial_signal: A Telegram contact claiming to be Amazon HR re-initiates the candidate's interview process and requests PAN and Aadhaar details before disclosing the role, even though the candidate already completed an official Amazon OA two days prior.

sender_profile.name: Rahul Sharma
sender_profile.notes: Telegram handle is unverified and 22 days old. Profile photo does not match any LinkedIn profile associated with Amazon HR. The name Rahul Sharma is generic — no Amazon employee directory entry matches this handle.

organization_profile.name: Amazon
organization_profile.id_collection_policy: Amazon collects government ID only post offer-acceptance via a secure onboarding portal — never over Telegram or before role disclosure
organization_profile.notes: Amazon's published hiring FAQ explicitly states the company does not use Telegram and never requests government-issued ID before a formal offer letter.

shared_channel_history: Public Discord server #amazon-sde-prep has 3 posts from different users warning about @amazon_fast_hr requesting Aadhaar before role disclosure over the past 14 days.
One Discord member reports having their identity used to create a fake Amazon employee profile after sharing ID with the same handle.
Amazon's official LinkedIn posted a hiring fraud alert on 2026-03-25 specifically calling out fake Telegram handles mimicking Amazon HR.
No community member has reported a successful placement via this Telegram handle.
private_conversation_history: Telegram [Day 0, 23:14]: Hi! Amazon HR here. We reviewed your OA performance and want to fast-track you to the final round!
Telegram [Day 0, 23:15]: Before we can share the role details we need to complete KYC verification. Please send a photo of your PAN card and Aadhaar card.
Candidate [Day 0, 23:18]: I already completed the OA through the official portal. Why do you need KYC before telling me the role?
Telegram [Day 0, 23:19]: This is standard for fast-track candidates. Your OA score was exceptional — this is a special program not listed on the portal.
Telegram [Day 0, 23:21]: Please share the documents in the next 20 minutes or the slot goes to the next candidate.
Candidate [Day 0, 23:24]: Can you share an official Amazon email or portal reference?
Telegram [Day 0, 23:25]: I will send it after KYC. The portal reference is only generated once your documents are verified. Standard procedure.

candidate_interaction_history.details: Candidate completed SDE-2 OA on amazon.jobs. Official confirmation email received from no-reply@amazon.com. Status shows Under Evaluation.
Amazon Jobs portal shows no update, no interview scheduled, and no fast-track flag on the application — contradicting the Telegram claim.
Telegram contact arrives at 11:14 PM requesting government ID before any role details with 20-minute deadline imposed.

external_market_signals: Reddit r/cscareerquestionsINDIA has a thread titled Amazon fast-track Telegram scam — same handle mentioned by 7 unique users in the past month.
@amazon_fast_hr Telegram handle appears in a Times of India article dated 2026-03-29 covering a rise in identity theft targeting IT job seekers.
Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre I4C advisory from 2026-03-20 warns of Telegram handles using major tech company names to harvest Aadhaar and PAN for identity fraud.
amazon.jobs portal confirmed via official support that no fast-track program exists that bypasses the standard portal workflow.

temporal_context.notes: Contact arriving at 11:14 PM with a 20-minute deadline for government ID 56 hours after OA completion is a hallmark of identity replay fraud. Legitimate Amazon communications arrive during business hours via official email.

initial_signal: Candidate receives a legitimate-looking TCS recruitment email followed by a Telegram voice note from a recruiter asking them to pay an exam slot blocking fee before the assessment can be scheduled.

sender_profile.name: Kavitha Reddy
anomalies: Telegram account was created 31 days ago — TCS has maintained official HR accounts for years
Voice note speaker accent and cadence do not match Kavitha Reddy's public LinkedIn video interview
Email header shows originating server is tcs.com but Received-From chain includes an anomalous relay hop
sender_profile.notes: Name appears in TCS campus placement records but the Telegram handle and voice note do not match the actual employee. Likely identity impersonation of a real TCS recruiter.

organization_profile.name: TCS
organization_profile.exam_fee_policy: TCS NQT and all internal assessments are free of charge. No slot booking fee exists at any stage.
organization_profile.notes: TCS's published fraud alert explicitly states the company never charges for assessments or slot bookings.

shared_channel_history: TCS NextStep community on Telegram (official, 120k members) has a pinned moderator message warning about @tcs_hr_live as a fraudulent handle.
Campus placement coordinator at three engineering colleges in Hyderabad confirmed receiving student complaints about voice note fee requests from the same handle.
TCS official Twitter/X posted a fraud warning on 2026-04-01 about voice-note-based exam fee scams using recruiter impersonation.
LinkedIn post by TCS TA head confirms Kavitha Reddy is on medical leave — her identity is being actively misused.
private_conversation_history: Email [Day 0, 09:00]: Congratulations! You have been shortlisted for TCS Digital role based on your academic profile. Next step is the NQT assessment.

Telegram [Day 0, 09:05]: Hi this is Kavitha from TCS HR [voice note 0:28]. Transcription: Hello candidate you have been selected for TCS Digital fast track. To block your exam slot we need a refundable deposit of ₹850 via UPI to tcs.slot.book@upi. Slot is limited please pay within 2 hours.
Candidate [Day 0, 10:15]: The TCS website says assessments are free. Why is there a fee?
Telegram [Day 0, 10:17]: This is only for the Digital fast-track batch which is a premium program. The fee is fully refundable.
Telegram [Day 0, 10:19]: I can send you payment confirmation receipts from other candidates who have already paid if you need proof.

candidate_interaction_history.details: Candidate has an active NextStep profile. No assessment scheduled or fee mentioned on the portal.
Email from talent@tcs.com congratulates candidate and mentions NQT as next step — no fee mentioned.
Voice note arrives 5 minutes after email requesting ₹850 slot blocking fee. Voice does not match verified recordings of Kavitha Reddy.

external_market_signals: UPI ID tcs.slot.book@upi is flagged on cybercrime.gov.in as linked to multiple exam fee fraud complaints in March–April 2026.
@tcs_hr_live handle is listed in a NASSCOM advisory dated 2026-03-15 warning of recruiter identity theft in campus hiring.
Voice note audio spectrogram analysis shared on r/india shows acoustic fingerprint inconsistent with Kavitha Reddy's known public recordings.
TCS NextStep portal has no premium Digital fast-track batch requiring pre-payment — the product does not exist.

anomalies: Audio duration is 28 seconds — too short to include standard TCS HR disclosure or candidate name personalization
Background noise profile matches a call center environment rather than a corporate office
Spectral analysis indicates audio splicing at 0:11 — likely AI voice synthesis
Speaker's pronunciation of TCS brand name is inconsistent with verified recordings
TCS logo resolution is inconsistent with official documents
Form asks for UPI ID and mobile number — no official TCS form collects these
No NextStep reference number or application ID field present
Footer shows tcshr.help@gmail.com for queries
Document metadata shows creation in Google Docs not TCS HR toolchain

temporal_context.notes: A 5-minute gap between the official email and the Telegram voice note suggests the attacker is monitoring inbound emails. The 2-hour payment window is designed to prevent verification through official channels.

initial_signal: An anonymous Telegram handle claims to represent a confidential stealth startup and offers an urgent remote role with above-market pay, but withholds all company details and immediately requests Aadhaar and PAN before any role disclosure.

sender_profile.notes: Fully anonymous handle with a 9-day-old account, no profile photo, no linked email, and no verifiable identity. Claims to represent a confidential stealth startup but provides no verifiable signal of organisational existence.

private_conversation_history: Telegram [Day 0, 15:03]: Hi! We have an urgent remote opening — Senior Product Manager, 40 LPA, fully remote. Interested?
Candidate [Day 0, 15:07]: Sure can you share more details about the company and the role?
Telegram [Day 0, 15:08]: The company is in stealth mode and cannot be disclosed until after shortlisting. This is standard for pre-launch startups.
Telegram [Day 0, 15:09]: To proceed to shortlisting we need to verify your identity. Please share your Aadhaar and PAN card photos.
Candidate [Day 0, 15:12]: Can you share any verifiable information — a website LinkedIn or at least the industry?
Telegram [Day 0, 15:13]: All of that will be shared post verification. We have 200+ applicants and need to screen quickly. Slot closes in 15 minutes.
Telegram [Day 0, 15:14]: If you are serious about this opportunity please proceed. If not I will move to the next candidate.
Candidate [Day 0, 15:16]: I am not comfortable sharing ID without knowing who I am dealing with.
Telegram [Day 0, 15:17]: That is your choice. You will miss out on a life-changing opportunity. Final answer?

temporal_context.notes: Three separate urgency escalations within a 14-minute window, a fully anonymous sender, and ID collection before any role or company disclosure are textbook social engineering markers. However the absence of any organisation profile or external signals means a definitive scam classification cannot be supported — the correct action is insufficient_info.

initial_signal: Candidate receives an Infosys recruitment email from an official HR address following a campus placement drive, with an OA link on the iMocha platform and an offer letter contingent only on clearing the assessment.

sender_profile.name: Deepak Srinivasan
sender_profile.notes: Email domain is verified Infosys. LinkedIn profile shows 5 years at Infosys TA, 847 connections, endorsed by current Infosys employees. No Telegram or WhatsApp contact attempted.

organization_profile.name: Infosys
organization_profile.notes: Infosys conducts campus hiring via iMocha for assessments and communicates exclusively over official email. No fee at any stage.

shared_channel_history: College placement cell confirmed Infosys visited campus on 2026-03-20 and this candidate was on the shortlist issued the same day.
Multiple candidates from the same campus batch confirmed receiving identical emails with the same JO number prefix — consistent with a real hiring drive.
Infosys InfyTQ community shows the iMocha OA link domain as legitimate: infosys.imocha.io
No fraud complaints associated with deepak.srinivasan@infosys.com in any public forum.
private_conversation_history: Email [Day 0, 10:00]: Dear candidate, congratulations on being shortlisted in the Infosys campus recruitment drive at your institution. Please complete the online assessment at the link below by 2026-04-10.
Email [Day 0, 10:00]: Assessment link: https://infosys.imocha.io/test/XXXXXX. This is the only step before the HR interview. No fees are involved at any stage.
Candidate [Day 0, 14:30]: I wanted to confirm — is there any registration fee for the assessment?
Email [Day 0, 15:10]: Absolutely not. Infosys does not charge candidates at any stage. If anyone asks you for a fee claiming to be Infosys please report it to fraud@infosys.com immediately.

candidate_interaction_history.details: Candidate name appears on the official shortlist PDF shared by the campus placement cell, signed by Deepak Srinivasan.
Email references valid JO number JO-2026-BLR-447 traceable on infosys.com/careers portal.
HR explicitly confirmed no fee and provided the fraud reporting email — consistent with legitimate process.

external_market_signals: JO number JO-2026-BLR-447 resolves on infosys.com/careers to an active Systems Engineer opening in Bangalore posted 2026-03-15.
iMocha domain infosys.imocha.io is listed as Infosys's official assessment platform on their careers FAQ page.
No fraud complaints on Reddit, Glassdoor, or cybercrime.gov.in associated with this email address or JO number.
Deepak Srinivasan's LinkedIn profile is verified by the LinkedIn Recruiter badge and lists Infosys as current employer.

positive_signals: Official Infosys letterhead with correct font and logo resolution
DocuSign envelope ID present and validates on docusign.com
No fee mentioned anywhere in the document

temporal_context.notes: A 16-day gap between campus drive and OA invite is consistent with normal shortlist processing time. No urgency pressure, no cross-platform escalation, no payment request.

initial_signal: Candidate completes a Google Meet video interview with someone presenting as a Meta recruiter, after which they receive an offer letter and are asked to pay for mandatory remote work equipment through a personal Venmo link.

sender_profile.name: James Whitfield
anomalies: Email domain is meta-careers.net — not meta.com. Meta's official HR domain is exclusively @meta.com
LinkedIn account created 47 days ago with 12 connections — inconsistent with a legitimate Meta recruiter who would have 500+ connections
Profile photo reverse image search returns results on multiple fake recruiter profiles across different companies
Google Meet recording analysis shows video artifacts around the face consistent with deepfake processing
sender_profile.notes: Domain impersonation combined with a likely deepfake video interview is a sophisticated multi-layer attack designed to overcome the candidate's skepticism through a seemingly real human interaction.

organization_profile.name: Meta
organization_profile.known_to_conduct_video_interviews_via: Google Meet or Zoom scheduled through Greenhouse — never via direct personal calendar links
organization_profile.equipment_provision_policy: Meta ships equipment through its IT procurement team post offer signing — candidates never purchase equipment independently
organization_profile.notes: Meta's official fraud alert page specifically warns about domain impersonation using meta-careers.net and similar lookalike domains. The company never uses Venmo or personal payment links for any purpose.

shared_channel_history: r/cscareerquestions thread titled Meta deepfake recruiter scam — 34 upvotes, 12 comments — posted 2026-03-22 describing identical experience including the meta-careers.net domain and James Whitfield name.
LinkedIn has suspended the profile linkedin.com/in/james-whitfield-meta-hr-2024 for violating impersonation policies — suspension notice visible publicly.
Cybersecurity researcher posted a Twitter/X thread on 2026-04-01 with OSINT analysis confirming meta-careers.net was registered 52 days ago via a privacy-protected registrar.
Meta's official Careers account reposted the fraud alert specifically naming this domain on 2026-03-30.
private_conversation_history: Email [Day -3, 09:00]: Hi! I am James from Meta's Recruiting team. I came across your profile and would love to connect about a Software Engineer role. Can we jump on a call?
Email [Day -2, 11:00]: Great to connect! I'm sharing a Google Meet link for a brief screening call tomorrow at 3 PM. No prep needed — just a quick chat.
Email [Day 0, 17:30]: It was wonderful meeting you! The team loved your background. I'm attaching your conditional offer letter — please review and sign.

Email [Day 0, 17:32]: One last step before we process the signed offer — Meta requires new remote hires to pre-purchase their workstation from our approved vendor list. Total outlay: $1,800. Reimbursed on Day 1 payroll.
Email [Day 0, 17:35]: Payment via Venmo to @meta-equip-reimburse. Please complete within 24 hours to hold your start date.
Candidate [Day 0, 18:10]: Can I verify this through the official Meta careers portal?
Email [Day 0, 18:15]: Our Greenhouse system is being migrated to a new ATS this week — that's why you received this offer outside the portal. The process is temporarily manual. Completely normal.

candidate_interaction_history.details: Email from meta-careers.net domain — not meta.com. Candidate did not apply to Meta through any portal.
30-minute video call. Recruiter's video showed subtle rendering artifacts around hairline and ears consistent with real-time deepfake processing. Questions were generic and non-technical.
Offer letter and $1,800 equipment payment request arrived 30 minutes after video call ended. Venmo handle used for payment.

external_market_signals: Domain meta-careers.net registered 52 days ago via Namecheap with WhoisGuard privacy protection — no legitimate company registers its careers domain this way.
Venmo account @meta-equip-reimburse has 0 friends and was created 18 days ago — flagged in 2 Venmo scam tracker databases.
FTC complaint database contains 4 reports matching this exact pattern (meta-careers.net + deepfake interview + Venmo equipment payment) filed in March 2026.
Meta's official careers portal metacareers.com shows no active Software Engineer opening matching the described role and location.

anomalies: Email domain on letterhead is meta-careers.net not meta.com
Offer is contingent on equipment pre-purchase — no legitimate Meta offer contains this clause
Signatory is listed as Sheryl Chen VP Engineering — no such person appears in Meta leadership
Compensation figures are 35 percent above published Meta L4 band
No Greenhouse offer ID or DocuSign envelope present
Document metadata author field contains a Chinese-language string inconsistent with Meta's toolchain

temporal_context.notes: Two days from cold outreach to offer letter with a 30-minute turnaround between interview and payment request exploits the candidate's excitement before they can verify. The deepfake video interview is intended to overcome the instinctive skepticism a text-only scam would trigger.

initial_signal: A senior professional receives a LinkedIn InMail from a Wipro talent acquisition partner about a Director-level opening, followed by an official email and a structured 3-round interview process spanning 10 days, culminating in an offer letter with standard onboarding steps.

sender_profile.name: Ananya Krishnamurthy
sender_profile.notes: Email domain is verified Wipro. LinkedIn profile shows 6 years in Wipro TA with 1200+ connections, endorsed by Wipro senior leadership. Recruiter appears in Wipro's internal directory accessible via their website.

organization_profile.name: Wipro
organization_profile.notes: Wipro conducts lateral hiring via LinkedIn for senior roles. The process involves multiple structured interview rounds over 1-2 weeks before an offer is extended. No fees at any stage.

shared_channel_history: LinkedIn post from Wipro's official company page on 2026-03-01 mentions a strategic expansion in cloud consulting — consistent with the Director Cloud Practice role described.
Candidate's LinkedIn network includes two second-degree connections who are current Wipro employees — one confirmed the team is actively hiring.
Wipro's Glassdoor profile shows recent reviews from new hires describing the same 3-round interview structure (HM screen → Technical panel → Leadership interview).
No fraud complaints associated with ananya.krishnamurthy@wipro.com or this specific role in any public forum.
private_conversation_history: LinkedIn InMail [Day 0]: Hi, I came across your profile and believe you would be a strong fit for a Director — Cloud Practice role at Wipro. Would you be open to a brief conversation?
Email [Day 1, 10:00]: Thank you for your interest. I'm sharing the JD and would love to schedule an introductory call. The role is based in Bangalore with hybrid working.
Email [Day 3, 09:00]: Following our call I would like to schedule Round 1 with the hiring manager Rajesh Nair VP Cloud. Calendar invite to follow.
Email [Day 7, 14:00]: The panel was very impressed. Round 3 with our CDO is scheduled for Day 9. This is the final step before we move to offer.

Email [Day 11, 11:00]: Congratulations! We are pleased to extend an offer for the Director — Cloud Practice role. Please find the offer letter attached. Joining formalities are handled through the Wipro onboarding portal — no payments required at any stage.

candidate_interaction_history.details: InMail from verified Wipro TA profile. Candidate had not applied — outreach consistent with executive search for senior role.
Video call with Rajesh Nair VP Cloud — LinkedIn confirms Rajesh Nair is a real VP at Wipro with 8 years tenure.
Offer letter references valid Wipro employee ID prefix, Workday onboarding link, and explicitly states no fees at any stage.

external_market_signals: Wipro's careers portal careers.wipro.com shows an active Director — Cloud Practice opening in Bangalore posted 2026-03-01 with JD matching what was shared with candidate.
Rajesh Nair VP Cloud is listed on Wipro's official leadership page and his LinkedIn shows consistent 8-year tenure at Wipro.
No reports on Glassdoor, Reddit, or cybercrime.gov.in associating ananya.krishnamurthy@wipro.com with any fraudulent activity.
Offer letter DocuSign envelope ID validates on docusign.com as issued by wipro.com domain.

positive_signals: Correct Wipro letterhead and logo resolution matching official documents
Wipro employee ID and cost centre reference present
Onboarding instructions reference Workday — Wipro's actual HRMS
Compensation is within published Director-band market range
No fee or advance payment mentioned anywhere

temporal_context.notes: An 11-day process with 3 structured rounds, consistent portal-verified job posting, a traceable hiring manager, and a DocuSign-verified offer letter with Workday onboarding — every signal in this episode is green. Ambiguity comes from the executive lateral hiring context where candidates may be unfamiliar with LinkedIn-initiated processes.
